Package project2_root.models

Class ProductUsageModel

java.lang.Object
project2_root.models.ProductUsageModel
All Implemented Interfaces:
Model
public class ProductUsageModel extends Object implements Model
Model for the Product Usage view.

Provides ingredient consumption data aggregated over a manager-specified date range, along with date range validation and formatting helpers.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ProductUsageModel(POSDatabase database)
    Constructs the model with the given database connection.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    getDateRangeLabel(LocalDate start, LocalDate end)
    Formats a date range as a human-readable label.
    Collection<IngredientUsageRow>
    getIngredientUsage(LocalDate start, LocalDate end)
    Returns total ingredient servings consumed by completed orders whose completion date falls within [start, end] inclusive, ordered by servings used descending.
    boolean
    isValidRange(LocalDate start, LocalDate end)
    Returns whether the given date range is valid for querying.

  • Constructor Details

    • ProductUsageModel

      public ProductUsageModel(POSDatabase database)
      Constructs the model with the given database connection.
      Parameters:
      database - the active POSDatabase instance

  • Method Details

    • getIngredientUsage

      public Collection<IngredientUsageRow> getIngredientUsage(LocalDate start, LocalDate end)
      Returns total ingredient servings consumed by completed orders whose completion date falls within [start, end] inclusive, ordered by servings used descending.
      Parameters:
      start - the inclusive start date of the query window
      end - the inclusive end date of the query window
      Returns:
      a collection of IngredientUsageRow records, one per ingredient

    • isValidRange

      public boolean isValidRange(LocalDate start, LocalDate end)
      Returns whether the given date range is valid for querying. A range is valid when both dates are non-null and start is not after end.
      Parameters:
      start - the candidate start date
      end - the candidate end date
      Returns:
      true if the range is valid

    • getDateRangeLabel

      public String getDateRangeLabel(LocalDate start, LocalDate end)
      Formats a date range as a human-readable label.
      Parameters:
      start - the start date
      end - the end date
      Returns:
      a string in the form "yyyy-MM-dd to yyyy-MM-dd"
